changes.value:
## What
Create a `ValidatorFileTool` in `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/` that exposes only `read file` and `glob files` operations. This is a non-agent tool (`is_agent_tool() -> false`) so it won't be stripped by `remove_agent_tools()`.

The existing `FilesTool` is marked `is_agent_tool=true` (it includes write/edit), so we need a separate read-only variant for the validator MCP server.

**Files:**
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/validator_read.rs` (new)
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/mod.rs` (register new tool)

**Approach:** Reuse existing `execute_read()` and `execute_glob()` from the files tool. The new tool just dispatches to those, minus write/edit operations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ValidatorFileTool` implements `McpTool` with `is_agent_tool() -> false`
- [ ] Only supports `read file` and `glob files` operations
- [ ] Returns error for any write/edit/grep attempts
- [ ] Schema only advertises read and glob operations

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test: read file succeeds
- [ ] Unit test: glob files succeeds  
- [ ] Unit test: write/edit operations rejected
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools`
Create read-only file tool for validators

\ No newline at end of file
+## What
Refactor `FilesTool` from a unit struct to an instance with configurable operations. This lets us create a read-only variant for validators without a whole new tool.

**Files:**
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/mod.rs` — main refactor
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/description.md` — may become unnecessary

**Approach:**
1. Add instance fields to `FilesTool`:
   - `operations: Vec<&'static dyn Operation>` — subset of FILE_OPERATIONS
   - `agent_only: bool` — controls `is_agent_tool()` return value
2. Add constructors:
   - `FilesTool::all()` — all 5 ops, `agent_only: true` (current behavior)
   - `FilesTool::read_only()` — only read+glob, `agent_only: false`
3. Change `description()` to aggregate from operation descriptions + examples instead of the static `description.md` file
4. Change `schema()` to use `self.operations` instead of `&FILE_OPERATIONS`
5. Change `execute()` to check `self.operations` before dispatching — reject ops not in the set
6. Update `register_file_tools()` to use `FilesTool::all()`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `FilesTool::all()` produces identical behavior to current (backward compat)
- [ ] `FilesTool::read_only()` only allows read+glob, rejects write/edit/grep
- [ ] `description()` aggregates from individual operation descriptions
- [ ] `schema()` only advertises operations in the instance's set
- [ ] `is_agent_tool()` returns the configured `agent_only` value

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test: all() schema includes all 5 ops
- [ ] Unit test: read_only() schema includes only read+glob
- [ ] Unit test: read_only() execute rejects write/edit
- [ ] Unit test: description() includes operation descriptions
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools`"
+</invoke>
\ No newline at end of file

+## What
+Create a `ValidatorFileTool` in `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/` that exposes only `read file` and `glob files` operations. This is a non-agent tool (`is_agent_tool() -> false`) so it won't be stripped by `remove_agent_tools()`.
+
+The existing `FilesTool` is marked `is_agent_tool=true` (it includes write/edit), so we need a separate read-only variant for the validator MCP server.
+
+**Files:**
+- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/validator_read.rs` (new)
+- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/mod.rs` (register new tool)
+
+**Approach:** Reuse existing `execute_read()` and `execute_glob()` from the files tool. The new tool just dispatches to those, minus write/edit operations.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `ValidatorFileTool` implements `McpTool` with `is_agent_tool() -> false`
+- [ ] Only supports `read file` and `glob files` operations
+- [ ] Returns error for any write/edit/grep attempts
+- [ ] Schema only advertises read and glob operations
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit test: read file succeeds
+- [ ] Unit test: glob files succeeds  
+- [ ] Unit test: write/edit operations rejected
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools`
